source.rationale: Deliberately reproduces the same SHAPE of gap found in the real case 001_starlette_gzip_compresslevel (Engineering Decision Log Entry 39): a code-example call site AND a separate parameter bullet both need updating in the same doc section, but the Corrector's design proposes exactly one contiguous span. Built by hand specifically to get a second, independent data point on this question without waiting on real-history mining to surface another one — one real + one deliberately-constructed instance is stronger evidence of how often this actually costs completeness than either alone.

stale_doc_section:
```python
create_backup("/data", "/backups")
```

Parameters:

* `source_path` — path to the directory to back up.
* `destination_path` — path where the backup will be written.
human_fixed_doc_section:
```python
create_backup("/data", "/backups", compress=True)
```

Parameters:

* `source_path` — path to the directory to back up.
* `destination_path` — path where the backup will be written.
* `compress` — optional. gzip-compress the backup contents. Defaults to `False`.

labels.notes_for_labeler: CONFIRMED (real run): proposed_new_text only added the new `compress` bullet — it did not touch the code example block, which still shows `create_backup("/data", "/backups")` without `compress=True`, exactly reproducing case 001's gap. This is now a SECOND independent confirmation (real-mined case 001 + this hand-authored case) of the single-span design's completeness cost — no longer a one-off. Strengthens the case for treating Future Product Opportunities #11 (multi-span Corrector support) as backed by a real, recurring pattern rather than a single data point, though still not enough instances (n=2) to justify the added complexity on its own.
